title: Riakporhe vs Billam-Smith: the puncher and the points machine at cruiserweight
summary: Two routes to the same elite level meet at cruiserweight. Richard Riakporhe ends fights. Chris Billam-Smith wins them in instalments.
body:
At cruiserweight, two opposite ways of reaching the same level collide. Richard Riakporhe ends fights. Chris Billam-Smith wins them in instalments. Their records sit close together in quality and a long way apart in character, which is what makes the matchup worth a look.

Riakporhe: power, and a single defeat

Riakporhe's 20-1 ledger is the heavier one. He has 16 knockouts, an 80 per cent finishing rate, and a recent five built around a three-fight stoppage streak. At 6ft 5in and 36 years old he is the bigger man in the ring and the one more likely to make the scorecards irrelevant.

Billam-Smith: output and the deep rounds

Billam-Smith, 35, gets there differently. His 22-2 record leans on activity. He has 14 knockouts at 64 per cent, plus eight wins on the cards and a recent run of decision victories that speaks to a fighter comfortable working twelve hard rounds. Where Riakporhe looks to shorten a fight, Billam-Smith is happy to let it run and out-work you in it.

How it splits

Two inches of height favour Riakporhe, and the power clearly does. Billam-Smith's counter is volume and his ease in the championship rounds. Does Riakporhe's power land before the busier man piles up the rounds, or does Billam-Smith drag the puncher somewhere he would rather not go? On the numbers, that is the fight worth tuning in for.
